The only difference is in row 0, which is the HUD bar. The pink bar (P) shrank by 1 character, and the charcoal bar (c) grew by 1 character. This is just the HUD/timer bar, not a gameplay change.

The pink bar (P) went from 54 chars to 53 chars, and the charcoal bar (c) went from 9 chars to 10 chars. This is just the HUD bar changing.

The black bars have gaps at:

- Col 36-37: row 39 (purple block is here)

So the colored blocks are 1 row below the black bar tops (41 vs 38, 39 vs 36, 47 vs 30... wait, 47 vs 30 is 17 rows, not 1 row).
Hmm, let me re-examine. The green block is at row 41, and the black bar top is at row 38. So the green block is 3 rows below the black bar top. The purple block is at row 39, and the black bar top is at row 36. So the purple block is 3 rows below the black bar top. The yellow block is at row 47, and the black bar top is at row 30. So the yellow block is 17 rows below the black bar top.
This doesn't seem to be a consistent pattern. Let me think about this differently.
Actually, I think the goal might be to move all colored blocks to the same row. Let me try clicking the blue blocks to move all colored blocks to row 30 (the topmost black bar).

Let me re-examine the movement. When I clicked (56,12), the leftmost column blocks moved from (51,8)-(52,10) to (49,8)-(50,10), which is a change of 2 rows. So the movement is indeed in steps of 2 rows.

Similarly, the purple block at (39,36)-(39,37) is at row 39, which is odd. If it moves up 2 rows, it goes to row 37, which is also odd.
And the yellow block at (47,48)-(47,49) is at row 47, which is odd. If it moves up 2 rows, it goes to row 45, which is also odd.

Let me try to move all colored blocks to row 39 (the purple block's current row). To do this:

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The only change was the HUD bar (row 0). Clicking on black bars doesn't change gameplay.
# Let me systematically test all blue blocks to map their effects.

# Current state colored blocks:
# - c at (27,52)-(28,54), Y at (29,52)-(29,54) - fifth column
# - p at (39,36)-(39,37) - third column
# - N at (41,14)-(41,15) - second column
# - c at (47,41)-(48,43), p at (49,41)-(49,43) - fourth column
# - Y at (47,48)-(47,49) - fifth column (inner)
# - c at (51,8)-(52,10), N at (53,8)-(53,10) - leftmost column

# Let me check what actions have been tried from state 10
print(f"Current state: {state_graph.current}")
print(f"Tried actions from current state: {state_graph.tried_actions(state_graph.current)}")

# Let me try clicking (56,20) which is in the second white column (cols 18-23)
# This might control the green block at (41,14)-(41,15)
print("\nTrying (56,20):")
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 56, 'col': 20}], intent='solve')
print(f"  state={state_graph.current}, completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
print(f"  gameplay_changed={last_action_result.get('gameplay_changed')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'N', 'p', 'Y'] and n['pixels'] <= 6:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {n['color']} at ({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
